parameters.name: sourcegrp
parameters.label: Source Group
parameters.description: Specifies a subset of input primitives to copy from. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: templategrp
parameters.label: Template Group
parameters.description: Specifies a subset of template primitives from which to copy onto. Accepts patterns, as described in .

parameters.name: ncy
parameters.label: Number of Copies
parameters.description: Sets number of Copies to be made of the source. For a template input, it specifies the number of copies to be placed at each point of the template.

parameters.name: nprims
parameters.label: Primitives per Point
parameters.description: Defines how many primitives to copy from each point.

parameters.name: nml
parameters.label: Rotate to Normal
parameters.description: Only used when a template input is specified. If the template is a sphere, and the first input is a circle, a circle is placed at each point of the sphere. With this option on, all the circles will re

parameters.name: cum
parameters.label: Transform Cumulative
parameters.description: Each transformation "builds" on the location left by the one before it. Transformations are cumulative as the Copy produces new copies.

parameters.name: xord
parameters.label: Transform Order
parameters.description: Sets the overall transform order for the transformations. The determines the order in which transformations take place. Depending on the order, you can achieve different results using the exact same v

parameters.name: rord
parameters.label: Rotate Order
parameters.description: Sets the order of the rotations within the overall transform order.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.label: Uniform Scale
parameters.description: Uniform Scale allows you to shrink or enlarge geometry along all three axes simultaneously.

parameters.name: vlength
parameters.label: Normals Maintain Length
parameters.description: Vector type attributes (i.e. normals, velocity) maintain the same length under transforms. i.e. When geometry is scaled, the normals remain constant in length.

parameters.name: newg
parameters.label: Create Output Groups
parameters.description: If selected, this creates a group for each copy number, and places each primitive created at that stage into it.

parameters.name: copyg
parameters.label: Copy Groups
parameters.description: Defines the base name of the groups created.

parameters.name: lookat
parameters.label: Look At
parameters.description: Orients the copied geometry to lookat, or point to, the component specified in the parameter.

parameters.name: stamp
parameters.label: Stamp Inputs
parameters.description: When enabled, it will Stamp proceeding variables for each input copied.

parameters.name: copy
parameters.label: Copy
parameters.description: Sequence of stamp variables

parameters.name: copy0param
parameters.label: Param
parameters.description: Token of each stamp variable. Stamped parameters are accessible via the global method in the in python, or in tscript. See the example, below.

parameters.name: copy0value
parameters.label: Value
parameters.description: Value of each stamp variable. Stamped parameters are accessible via the global method in the in python, or in tscript. See the example, below.

parameters.name: doattr
parameters.label: Use Template Point Attribs
parameters.description: Enables the parameters below to allow template point attributes to be applied to the copied source geometry.

parameters.name: setpt
parameters.label: Copy to Point
parameters.description: Copy the attributes to the source geometry's points.

parameters.name: setprim
parameters.label: Copy to Primitive
parameters.description: Copy the attributes to the source geometry's primitives.

parameters.name: setvtx
parameters.label: Copy to Vertex
parameters.description: Copy the attributes to the source geometry's vertices.

parameters.name: mulpt
parameters.label: Multiply Point
parameters.description: Multiply the attributes with the source geometry's point attributes.

parameters.name: mulprim
parameters.label: Multiply Primitive
parameters.description: Multiply the attributes with the source geometry's primitive attributes.

parameters.name: mulvtx
parameters.label: Multiply Vertex
parameters.description: Multiply the attributes with the source geometry's vertex attributes.

parameters.name: addpt
parameters.label: Add Point
parameters.description: Add the attributes to the source geometry's point attributes.

parameters.name: addprim
parameters.label: Add Primitive
parameters.description: Add the attributes to the source geometry's primitive attributes.

parameters.name: addvtx
parameters.label: Add Vertex
parameters.description: Add the attributes to the source geometry's vertex attributes.

parameters.name: subpt
parameters.label: Subtract Point
parameters.description: Subtract the attributes from the source geometry's point attributes.

parameters.name: subprim
parameters.label: Subtract Primitive
parameters.description: Subtract the attributes from the source geometry's primitive attributes.

parameters.name: subvtx
parameters.label: Subtract Vertex
parameters.description: Subtract the attributes from the source geometry's vertex attributes.

parameters.name: upvectorz
parameters.label: Upvectorz
parameters.description: When specifying a Look At, it is possible to specify an up vector for the lookat. Without using an up vector, it is possible to get poor animation when the lookat object passes through the Y axis of t

parameters.name: sz
parameters.label: Sz
parameters.description: These allow you to specify the Translation (how much it moves over in a given direction), Rotation, and the Scale between each copy. Three columns are given for X, Y, and Z coordinates. Guide geometry

parameters.name: pz
parameters.label: Pz
parameters.description: These allow you to specify the Translation (how much it moves over in a given direction), Rotation, and the Scale between each copy. Three columns are given for X, Y, and Z coordinates. Guide geometry

parameters.name: rz
parameters.label: Rz
parameters.description: These allow you to specify the Translation (how much it moves over in a given direction), Rotation, and the Scale between each copy. Three columns are given for X, Y, and Z coordinates. Guide geometry

parameters.name: tz
parameters.label: Tz
parameters.description: These allow you to specify the Translation (how much it moves over in a given direction), Rotation, and the Scale between each copy. Three columns are given for X, Y, and Z coordinates. Guide geometry

examples.label: Configure Number of Copies and Tx
examples.description: Common settings from 16 real Derivative examples
